key_insight: A graph-based 3D spatio-temporal model can learn breast MRI treatment trajectories to better predict pathological complete response after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could help personalize treatment planning by identifying patients more likely to respond to neoadjuvant therapy.
method: Uses a state-of-the-art graph neural network to model relationships across longitudinal imaging timepoints.
Adds three self-supervised objectives to learn complementary treatment-trajectory representations.
Evaluates the effect of scan count and inter-scan time differences on prediction performance.
evidence_signal: In 585 patients from the public ISPY-2 dataset, the method outperformed vision and self-supervised baselines across multiple classification metrics.
watch_item: Performance was shown on one public cohort, so external validation and real-world deployment testing are still needed.
paper_title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction
